(Redirected from [Samuel Dinsmoor, Jr.](/w/index.php?title=Samuel_Dinsmoor,_Jr.&redirect=no "Samuel Dinsmoor, Jr."))

American politician (1799–1869)

**Samuel Dinsmoor Jr.** (May 8, 1799 – February 24, 1869) was an American [lawyer](/wiki/Lawyer "Lawyer"), [banker](/wiki/Banker "Banker"), [politician](/wiki/Politician "Politician"), and the 22nd [governor of New Hampshire](/wiki/Governor_of_New_Hampshire "Governor of New Hampshire").

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Samuel_Dinsmoor_Jr.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Dinsmoor was born in [Keene, New Hampshire](/wiki/Keene,_New_Hampshire "Keene, New Hampshire") and graduated from [Dartmouth College](/wiki/Dartmouth_College "Dartmouth College") in 1814. He studied law and was a legal assistant to Territorial Governor [James Miller](/wiki/James_Miller_(general) "James Miller (general)") for several years in [Arkansas](/wiki/Arkansas "Arkansas").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Samuel_Dinsmoor_Jr.&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

A commissioner who made it possible for the visit of French General [Lafayette](/wiki/Marquis_de_Lafayette "Marquis de Lafayette") to New Hampshire in 1825, Dinsmoor also served as clerk of the [New Hampshire Senate](/wiki/New_Hampshire_Senate "New Hampshire Senate") in 1826, 1827, 1829 and 1830.

Having secured the [Democratic](/wiki/Democratic_Party_(United_States) "Democratic Party (United States)") gubernatorial nomination, Dinsmoor was elected by a popular vote in 1849, and reelected to a second term in 1850, as well as a third term in 1851. He served as thirtieth [Governor of New Hampshire](/wiki/Governor_of_New_Hampshire "Governor of New Hampshire")[[2]](#cite_note-2) from June 7, 1849 to June 3, 1852. The state militia was restructured during his tenure.

Upon leaving the governorship, Dinsmoor retired from political life, but continued to stay active in his legal and banking interests. From 1835 until his death Dinsmoor was President of the Ashuelot Bank in Keene.

## Death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Samuel_Dinsmoor_Jr.&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Death")]

Dinsmoor died in Keene on February 24, 1869 (age 69 years, 292 days). He is [interred](/wiki/Burial "Burial") at Washington Street Cemetery in Keene.

## Family life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Samuel_Dinsmoor_Jr.&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Family life")]

His father, [Samuel Dinsmoor](/wiki/Samuel_Dinsmoor "Samuel Dinsmoor"), had been [Governor of New Hampshire](/wiki/Governor_of_New_Hampshire "Governor of New Hampshire") from 1831 to 1834. On September 11, 1841, Samuel Dinsmoor Jr. married Anne Eliza Jarvis, and they had two children: William Jarvis Dinsmoor and Samuel Dinsmoor III. Anne died on July 17, 1849; and he married Catharine Pickman Abbott Fox on May 4, 1853.[[3]](#cite_note-3)
